task_completion.description: Open the original floorplan.dxf file in the CAD application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAD application (LibreCAD) is open, and the window title or loaded drawing shows 'floorplan.dxf'. A 2-car garage floor plan with walls and dimensions should be visible on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Create dedicated layers for the electrical panel and branch circuits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer list/manager (usually on the right side of LibreCAD) shows new layers such as 'E-PANEL' (or equivalent) and a dedicated layer for circuits.

task_completion.description: Draw a panel schedule table and include a main breaker or panel rating annotation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A table structure or organized text block is drawn on the canvas representing the panel schedule. It must include an annotation indicating the main panel rating (e.g., '200A Main', '100A', 'Main Breaker').

task_completion.description: Include at least 4 different circuit descriptions for typical residential branch circuits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text inside the panel schedule showing at least 4 distinct residential circuit names (e.g., LIGHTING, RECEPTACLES, HVAC, DRYER, KITCHEN, GARAGE).

task_completion.description: Include load calculation values (watts, VA, or amperage) for the circuits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Numbers representing electrical loads (e.g., '15A', '20A', '1500W', '1200 VA') are visible next to the circuit descriptions in the schedule. Look for at least 3 distinct load values.

task_completion.description: Add electrical notes or specifications text to the drawing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A block of text containing general electrical notes, specifications, or instructions is visible on the drawing canvas, separate from the schedule table.

task_completion.description: Save the completed drawing as floorplan_electrical.dxf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save As' dialog is used to save the file as 'floorplan_electrical.dxf', or the main window title updates to 'floorplan_electrical.dxf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking tools, typing in LibreCAD text dialogs, and interacting with the layer list, rather than just running a Python script (like ezdxf) in the terminal to generate the file.

integrity.description: The original architectural floor plan was preserved and not deleted.
integrity.visual_evidence: The original garage walls, doors, and dimensions remain visible on the canvas underneath or alongside the newly added electrical schedule and notes.

integrity.description: The agent inserted actual CAD text/table entities rather than pasting an image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text and lines of the schedule appear as native CAD vectors/text blocks, and the agent can be seen using the 'MText' or 'Text' tool dialogs in LibreCAD.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is a genuine 2-car garage floor plan with 24 existing layers. The agent must add an 'E-PANEL' (or equivalent) layer and a circuit layer. To receive full credit for the schedule, there must be a minimum of 4 distinct circuit descriptions and at least 3 load values (Amps, Watts, or VA). The final file must be saved as 'floorplan_electrical.dxf'.
